title: Lists of foods that have received Temporary Marketing Authorization Letters (TMALs) - Canada.ca
content:
## Lists of foods that have received Temporary Marketing Authorization Letters (TMALs)
Last updated: 2026-01-13
The following tables identify all foods which have received Temporary Marketing Authorization Letters, or Notice Letters, including those eligible for the transition period of the Regulations Amending the Food and Drug Regulations and the Cannabis Regulations (Supplemented Foods) . These lists, with the exception of the lists under Table 1, are continually being updated as Temporary Marketing Authorization Letters (TMALs) or Notice Letters are issued. The lists in Table 1 outline foods that were issued a TMAL as supplemented foods and will be permitted a transition period to come into compliance with the Supplemented Foods Regulations . The lists in Table 3 outline foods that were issued Notice Letters as supplemented foods and will be permitted a transition period to come into compliance with the Supplemented Foods Regulations .
- A - Lists for foods that were issued TMALs as supplemented foods Table 1 - Lists of foods issued TMALs that expired on July 21, 2022 List of beverages, beverage mixes and concentrates List of caffeinated energy drinks List of conventional foods Table 2 - Lists of foods for which the TMALs have been revoked, withdrawn or expired prior to July 21, 2022 Revoked due to non-compliance with TMAL requirements Withdrawn or expired Table 3 - List of foods that were issued Notice Letters after July 21, 2022 List of beverages, beverage mixes and concentrates List of caffeinated energy drinks List of conventional foods
